text: I can't stop worrying about my performance review next week. I keep imagining the worst outcomes.
My heart races every time my phone rings. I'm terrified it's bad news.
I lie awake at night running through everything that could go wrong with the project.
I've been avoiding social gatherings lately. The thought of small talk makes me feel sick.
Every morning I wake up with a knot in my stomach. I don't even know why.
I'm constantly checking my email even on weekends. I can't switch off.
My hands shake during presentations even though I've done hundreds of them.
I worry about my parents' health constantly. The uncertainty is unbearable.
I overthink every message I send. What if I said something wrong?
The thought of failing the UPSC again keeps me up at night. I'm terrified.
I've been having headaches from tension. My shoulders are permanently knotted.
I feel anxious even on my days off. Like something is always about to go wrong.
I'm scared of losing my job if I take a mental health day.
My mind never quiets. There's always a background hum of worry.
I panic in traffic jams. The feeling of being trapped is overwhelming.
The financial pressure of my EMI payments keeps me in a constant state of dread.
I've started avoiding my manager in the corridor because I'm anxious about feedback.
I get butterflies just thinking about the team meeting where I have to present.
I can't relax on vacation. I keep thinking about what's happening at work.
I've been checking the locks multiple times before I sleep. I know it's irrational.
Since my father passed, I cry at random moments throughout the day. Even at work.
I feel like a hollow version of myself. Nothing brings me joy anymore.
I miss my friends from college. We've all grown apart and I feel so lonely.
I moved to Bangalore for the job but I miss my family terribly. It hurts.
Everything reminds me of my ex. I keep replaying our memories.
My dog died last month. I still reach for him when I come home.
I've been crying in the shower every morning. I don't want my family to see me upset.
I feel like my best years are behind me and I didn't make the most of them.
I've lost interest in cooking, which I used to love. Nothing excites me anymore.
The loneliness of living alone in a new city is hitting me harder than I expected.
Sometimes I just sit and stare at nothing. I don't even feel sad. Just empty.
My mother is getting older and watching her slow down breaks my heart.
I grieve the relationship I had with my father before his dementia progressed.
I see my colleagues getting promoted and I feel left behind. It's a kind of grief.
My best friend moved abroad. The friendship is the same but the distance hurts.
I keep getting passed over for promotions despite working harder than anyone.
My teammates got increments but my rating was average. I felt humiliated and sad.
I worked on this project for 8 months and management cancelled it without explanation. I feel crushed.
I feel invisible at work. My contributions are never acknowledged.
My husband and I had a big fight and said things we can't take back. I'm heartbroken.
My manager humiliated me in front of the entire team. I wanted to disappear.
I've been overlooked for a promotion that was given to someone less qualified. I'm furious.
My company changed our leave policy without any consultation. I'm seething.
My in-laws make decisions about my home without asking me. I'm so angry.
My colleague takes credit for my ideas in meetings. I'm done being patient.
I worked 80-hour weeks and got a 3% raise while the CEO got a bonus. Rage.
I told my manager about a personal crisis and he told me to 'stay professional'. Unbelievable.
My father keeps telling me engineering was the wrong choice. I've heard it my whole life.
My partner dismisses my feelings every time I try to talk. I feel invisible.
I was blamed for a team failure that was clearly not my fault. I'm livid.
I'm scared I'll never be financially stable. The uncertainty is terrifying.
I'm afraid of disappointing my parents. They sacrificed everything for my education.
I'm terrified of what a cancer diagnosis might mean for my future.
I'm scared my mental health issues will affect my career if people find out.
My startup is failing and I'm afraid of having to go back home and face my family.
I'm scared to speak up about the injustice in my team in case I get fired.
I'm terrified of being alone. My last relationship ended and I don't know who I am.
I'm afraid I'm becoming my father — distant and cold. I don't want that.
I'm scared of dying young like my brother did. The fear is constant.
I'm terrified of commitment. Every time a relationship gets serious I pull away.
I feel like there's no way out of my current situation. Nothing I try works.
I've been trying for a year to get a better job. Nothing is working. I'm giving up.
My depression treatment doesn't seem to be working. I'm starting to think nothing will.
I can't see a future where things are better. The hopelessness is suffocating.
I've tried everything my therapist suggested. Nothing sticks. I feel defeated.
I feel stuck in a life that isn't mine. I don't know how to change it.
Sometimes I wonder what the point of all this effort is. Nothing changes.
I've been depressed for so long I've forgotten what normal feels like.
I can't imagine ever being happy. That sounds dramatic but it's how I feel.
I keep trying to be a better person and failing. I'm exhausted by my own expectations.
I failed my UPSC exam three times. I feel like I'm not meant to succeed.
My business failed after 4 years. I don't know if I have the strength to start again.
I let my parents down by dropping out of CA. I carry that guilt every day.
I wasn't there when my mother had her heart attack. I was traveling for work.
I said terrible things to my partner during a fight. I can't forgive myself.
I chose career over family for years. Now my children are grown and don't need me.
I haven't called my parents in weeks. I know they're waiting. I feel awful.
I took credit for a junior's work once. It still haunts me.
I've been lying to my family about how I'm really doing. I feel dishonest.
I make mistakes at work and punish myself mentally for days after.
I feel guilty for taking a day off when the team is under pressure.
I should have spoken up when I saw the harassment. I didn't. I hate myself for that.
I feel so embarrassed about my weight. I hate seeing myself in photos.
I failed publicly. My startup failed and everyone in my network knows.
I was made to feel stupid in front of the team. I still cringe when I think about it.
I can't let anyone know I'm struggling. In my family it's seen as weakness.
I come from a small town. I feel like I don't belong in these corporate spaces.
I cried in front of my manager. I can't stop thinking about how unprofessional that was.
My teeth are bad and I can't afford to fix them. I hate smiling.
I dropped out of IIT. In my community that's a mark I'll never escape.
I struggle with reading fast in a meeting. I feel exposed whenever I have to read aloud.
I feel inferior to my peers who went to top colleges and have fancy jobs.
